Main article: [Dominican Republic cuisine](/wiki/Dominican_Republic_cuisine "Dominican Republic cuisine")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/22/ChicharronMixto.JPG/250px-ChicharronMixto.JPG)](/wiki/File:ChicharronMixto.JPG)

Chicharrón mixto, a common dish in the country derived from Andalusia in southern Spain

Dominican cuisine is predominantly [Spanish](/wiki/Spanish_cuisine#Spanish_regional_variation "Spanish cuisine"), [Taíno](/wiki/Ta%C3%ADno#Food_and_agriculture "Taíno"), and [African](/wiki/African_cuisine "African cuisine") in origin. The typical cuisine is similar to what can be found in other Latin American countries.[[302]](#cite_note-308) One breakfast dish consists of eggs and *[mangú](/wiki/Mang%C3%BA_(dish) "Mangú (dish)")* (mashed, boiled plantain). Heartier versions of *mangú* are accompanied by deep-fried meat (Dominican salami, typically), cheese, or both. Lunch, generally the largest and most important meal of the day, usually consists of rice, meat, beans, and salad. "La Bandera" (literally "The Flag") is the most popular lunch dish; it consists of meat and red beans on white rice. *[Sancocho](/wiki/Sancocho "Sancocho")* is a stew often made with seven varieties of meat. [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Meals tend to favor meats and starches over dairy products and vegetables. Many dishes are made with *[sofrito](/wiki/Sofrito "Sofrito")*, which is a mix of local herbs used as a wet rub for meats and sautéed to bring out all of a dish's flavors. Throughout the south-central coast, [bulgur](/wiki/Bulgur "Bulgur"), or whole wheat, is a main ingredient in [*quipes* or *tipili*](/wiki/Tabbouleh "Tabbouleh") (bulgur salad). Other favorite Dominican foods include *[chicharrón](/wiki/Chicharr%C3%B3n "Chicharrón")*, *[yuca](/wiki/Cassava "Cassava")*, *[casabe](/wiki/Tapioca "Tapioca")*, *[pastelitos](/wiki/Cuban_pastry "Cuban pastry")* ([empanadas](/wiki/Empanada "Empanada")), *[batata](/wiki/Sweet_potato "Sweet potato")*, [*ñame*](/wiki/Yam_(vegetable) "Yam (vegetable)"), *[pasteles en hoja](/wiki/Pasteles "Pasteles")*, *[chimichurris](/wiki/Chimichurris "Chimichurris")*, and *[tostones](/wiki/Tostones "Tostones")*.

Some treats Dominicans enjoy are *[arroz con leche](/wiki/Rice_pudding "Rice pudding")* (or *arroz con dulce*), *[bizcocho dominicano](/wiki/Bizcocho_Dominicano "Bizcocho Dominicano")* (lit. 'Dominican cake'), *[habichuelas con dulce](/wiki/Habichuelas_con_dulce "Habichuelas con dulce")*, [flan](/wiki/Cr%C3%A8me_caramel "Crème caramel"), *[frío frío](/wiki/Snow_cone "Snow cone")* (snow cones), [dulce de leche](/wiki/Dulce_de_leche "Dulce de leche"), and *caña* ([sugarcane](/wiki/Sugarcane "Sugarcane")). The beverages Dominicans enjoy are *[Morir Soñando](/wiki/Morir_So%C3%B1ando "Morir Soñando")*, [rum](/wiki/Rum "Rum"), beer, *[Mama Juana](/wiki/Mama_Juana "Mama Juana")*,[[303]](#cite_note-309) *batidas* (smoothie), *jugos naturales* (freshly squeezed fruit juices), *[mabí](/wiki/Mauby "Mauby")*, coffee, and *[chaca](/w/index.php?title=Chaca_(dessert)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Chaca (dessert) (page does not exist)")* (also called *maiz caqueao/casqueado*, *maiz con dulce* and *maiz con leche*), the last item being found only in the southern provinces of the country such as San Juan.

In 2018 and 2019, Santo Domingo was named a Culinary Capital of the Caribbean by the Ibero-American Academy of Gastronomy.[[304]](#cite_note-310)

### Sports

Main article: [Sport in the Dominican Republic](/wiki/Sport_in_the_Dominican_Republic "Sport in the Dominican Republic")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0c/Albert_Pujols_%28Busch_Stadium%2C_2008%29.jpg/250px-Albert_Pujols_%28Busch_Stadium%2C_2008%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Albert_Pujols_(Busch_Stadium,_2008).jpg)

Dominican native and [Major League Baseball](/wiki/Major_League_Baseball "Major League Baseball") player [Albert Pujols](/wiki/Albert_Pujols "Albert Pujols")

[Baseball](/wiki/Baseball "Baseball") is by far the most popular sport in the Dominican Republic.[[298]](#cite_note-Harvey2006-304): 59 The [Dominican Professional Baseball League](/wiki/Dominican_Professional_Baseball_League "Dominican Professional Baseball League") consists of six teams. Its season usually begins in October and ends in January. After the United States, the Dominican Republic has the second-highest number of [Major League Baseball](/wiki/Major_League_Baseball "Major League Baseball") (MLB) players. [Ozzie Virgil Sr.](/wiki/Ozzie_Virgil_Sr. "Ozzie Virgil Sr.") became the first Dominican-born player in MLB on September 23, 1956. As of 2024, five Dominican-born players—[Adrián Beltré](/wiki/Adri%C3%A1n_Beltr%C3%A9 "Adrián Beltré"), [Vladimir Guerrero](/wiki/Vladimir_Guerrero "Vladimir Guerrero"), [Juan Marichal](/wiki/Juan_Marichal "Juan Marichal"), [Pedro Martínez](/wiki/Pedro_Mart%C3%ADnez "Pedro Martínez"), and [David Ortiz](/wiki/David_Ortiz "David Ortiz")—have been elected to the [Baseball Hall of Fame](/wiki/National_Baseball_Hall_of_Fame_and_Museum "National Baseball Hall of Fame and Museum").[[305]](#cite_note-311) Other notable baseball players born in the Dominican Republic are [José Bautista](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Bautista "José Bautista"), [Robinson Canó](/wiki/Robinson_Can%C3%B3 "Robinson Canó"), [Rico Carty](/wiki/Rico_Carty "Rico Carty"), [Bartolo Colón](/wiki/Bartolo_Col%C3%B3n "Bartolo Colón"), [Nelson Cruz](/wiki/Nelson_Cruz "Nelson Cruz"), [Edwin Encarnación](/wiki/Edwin_Encarnaci%C3%B3n "Edwin Encarnación"), [Cristian Javier](/wiki/Cristian_Javier "Cristian Javier"), [Ubaldo Jiménez](/wiki/Ubaldo_Jim%C3%A9nez "Ubaldo Jiménez"), [Francisco Liriano](/wiki/Francisco_Liriano "Francisco Liriano"), [Plácido Polanco](/wiki/Pl%C3%A1cido_Polanco "Plácido Polanco"), [Albert Pujols](/wiki/Albert_Pujols "Albert Pujols"), [Hanley Ramírez](/wiki/Hanley_Ram%C3%ADrez "Hanley Ramírez"), [Manny Ramírez](/wiki/Manny_Ram%C3%ADrez "Manny Ramírez"), [José Reyes](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Reyes_(infielder) "José Reyes (infielder)"), [Alfonso Soriano](/wiki/Alfonso_Soriano "Alfonso Soriano"), [Sammy Sosa](/wiki/Sammy_Sosa "Sammy Sosa"), [Juan Soto](/wiki/Juan_Soto "Juan Soto"), [Fernando Tatís Jr.](/wiki/Fernando_Tat%C3%ADs_Jr. "Fernando Tatís Jr."), [Miguel Tejada](/wiki/Miguel_Tejada "Miguel Tejada"), [Framber Valdez](/wiki/Framber_Valdez "Framber Valdez"), and [Elly De La Cruz](/wiki/Elly_De_La_Cruz "Elly De La Cruz"). [Felipe Alou](/wiki/Felipe_Alou "Felipe Alou") has also enjoyed success as a manager[[306]](#cite_note-312) and [Omar Minaya](/wiki/Omar_Minaya "Omar Minaya") as a general manager. In 2013, the Dominican team went undefeated *en route* to winning the [World Baseball Classic](/wiki/World_Baseball_Classic "World Baseball Classic").

In [boxing](/wiki/Boxing "Boxing"), the country has produced scores of world-class fighters and several world champions,[[307]](#cite_note-313) such as [Carlos Cruz](/wiki/Carlos_Cruz_(boxer) "Carlos Cruz (boxer)"), his brother [Leo](/wiki/Leo_Cruz "Leo Cruz"), [Juan Guzman](/wiki/Juan_Guzman_(boxer) "Juan Guzman (boxer)"), and [Joan Guzman](/wiki/Joan_Guzman "Joan Guzman").

Basketball also enjoys a relatively high level of popularity. [Tito Horford](/wiki/Tito_Horford "Tito Horford"), his son [Al](/wiki/Al_Horford "Al Horford"), [Felipe Lopez](/wiki/Felipe_L%C3%B3pez_(basketball) "Felipe López (basketball)"), and [Francisco Garcia](/wiki/Francisco_Garc%C3%ADa_(basketball) "Francisco García (basketball)") are among the Dominican-born players currently or formerly in the [National Basketball Association](/wiki/National_Basketball_Association "National Basketball Association") (NBA).

Olympic gold medalist and world champion hurdler [Félix Sánchez](/wiki/F%C3%A9lix_S%C3%A1nchez_(hurdler) "Félix Sánchez (hurdler)") hails from the Dominican Republic, as do former [NFL](/wiki/National_Football_League "National Football League") [defensive end](/wiki/Defensive_end "Defensive end") [Luis Castillo](/wiki/Luis_Castillo_(American_football) "Luis Castillo (American football)") and 2020 World and European Cyclo-cross champion [Ceylin del Carmen Alvarado](/wiki/Ceylin_del_Carmen_Alvarado "Ceylin del Carmen Alvarado").[[308]](#cite_note-314)

Other important sports are [volleyball](/wiki/Volleyball "Volleyball"), introduced in 1916 by U.S. Marines and controlled by the [Dominican Volleyball Federation](/wiki/Dominican_Volleyball_Federation "Dominican Volleyball Federation"), [taekwondo](/wiki/Taekwondo "Taekwondo"), in which [Gabriel Mercedes](/wiki/Gabriel_Mercedes "Gabriel Mercedes") won an Olympic silver medal in 2008, and [judo](/wiki/Judo "Judo").[[309]](#cite_note-315)

(...) En menor grado, también se está recabando información de los inmigrantes procedentes de Siria y Palestina.
